name: Almond Butter Fingers (Mordegspinner)
recipeInstructions:
DESCRIPTION: Nut-crusted, buttery almond bars.
Preheat oven to 275°F. Lightly grease a 10 x 15 jellyroll pan (9X13 cake pan will do). In a large mixing bowl, beat together the butter, powdered sugar, and salt until light and fluffy. Beat in the egg yolk and extracts (almond, vanilla, and cardamom). Stir in half of the flour (1 cup) at a time, blending well after each addition. Spread the dough evenly over the prepared pan. In a small bowl, lightly whisk the egg white until frothy. Pour over the dough, spreading evenly. Sprinkle the nuts over the dough and press lightly into the surface with the back of a spoon. Bake for 50-60 minutes or until golden brown. Immediately use a sharp knife to cut the bars into 40 "fingers" (4 across, 10 down). Cool on a rack for 15 minutes in the pan, then carefully remove from the pan. The cookies are fragile, so handle them gently. Allow them to cool completely on racks before storing in an airtight container for up to one week. If well-wrapped, they can be stored for three months in the freezer.
